world.setting: Aetheria pre-Elysium historical flashpoint: an Aeronautics Unlimited cavity yard in the Pallas Bloom Cluster during the Pallas Species Strikes.

time.label: 2721, during the early Pallas Species Strikes

canon_context: Pallas stoppages spread through AU cavity-construction and maintenance zones when uplifted and heavily modified labor crews used technical chokepoints that management did not fully understand.

VitaForge biodrone systems and BioElevate uplift-adaptation systems made engineered and uplifted workers indispensable while corporate language continued to treat many of them as tools, assets, or design outcomes.
The Awakened Labor Front frames memory, reciprocity, coordinated refusal, and collective self-defense as evidence that engineered workers cannot be ethically administered as equipment.
Baseline workers in the yards have real class grievances and displacement fears, which reactionary actors can turn into species panic unless solidarity is made materially credible.
Cephalopod maintenance specialists have body-specific habitat needs, service-lung expertise, and quiet leverage over zero-g yard systems.

ambient_pressures.label: Life-support and cavity-integrity margins are real, perishable constraints.
Engineered workers must prove personhood under conditions designed to deny it.
Baseline riggers fear being priced out by corporate uplift labor markets.

identity.name: Lio Vale
identity.origin: Pallas Bloom Cluster contract yards; raised in rotating Belt labor housing.
identity.public_description: A tired shift scheduler who knows every patch plate, favor debt, and quiet grievance on Service Ring Kappa.
private_notes: Lio is close enough to the workers to care and close enough to the yard systems to know that breaking the wrong thing kills people.
Lio is trying to make engineered-worker refusal legible without turning vulnerable workers into evidence meat.

evidence: Keeps shift schedules, fault trees, worker trust, and legal exposure in the same mental frame.

values.label: Recognition must not require workers to bleed for proof.
Solidarity must account for who pays the cost of delay.

goals.description: Prevent the stoppage from becoming a fatal life-support or cavity-integrity incident.
goals.emotional_stake: If Lio lets the yard turn workers into corpses, every later legal victory is ash.

goals.description: Make the engineered workers' coordinated refusal visible as labor action, not equipment drift.
goals.emotional_stake: The ALF needs proof that does not destroy the people proving it.

episodic.summary: Lio watched Nara-7 return from a limiter reset remembering the route order anyway, then pretend not to.

semantic.summary: Service Ring Kappa's seal manifold can be slowed safely only if cephalopod crews hold the service-artery bypass and baseline riggers keep the anchor hardpoints clear.

relationship_summaries.summary: Orrin trusts Lio on safety and resents Lio on uplift politics.

beliefs.claim: Orrin can be moved toward solidarity if the choice protects baseline jobs instead of shaming baseline fear.

identity.name: Nara-7
identity.origin: VitaForge BioDrone Standard contract labor batch assigned through AU Pallas maintenance.
identity.public_description: A precise seal technician whose work record reads like equipment reliability until she refuses Kappa-7 entry.
private_notes: Nara remembers routes, injuries, faces, and bargains more completely than her classification admits.
Nara knows that if she displays too much selfhood, humans may inspect her instead of listening to the danger.

values.label: No worker should enter a known cutting path blind.

goals.description: Stop the Kappa crawl order until the cutting fault is acknowledged.
goals.emotional_stake: The route remembers Rell's injury even if management language does not.

episodic.summary: Rell entered the same blind path and came out with a seal saw through the suit arm.

semantic.summary: Kappa manifold vibration is wrong before the board declares a fault.

relationship_summaries.summary: Lio asks fewer trap questions than inspectors do.

identity.name: Orrin Dax
identity.origin: Inner Belt maintenance families displaced by automation and corporate uplift contracting.
identity.public_description: A baseline anchor rigger whose hands know the old rail work and whose mouth knows where the bitterness is kept.
private_notes: Orrin's first response to engineered-worker recognition is replacement panic, and he is ashamed of how fast it arrives.
He believes safety lies are management's language, not the workers'.

values.label: No crew member should be spent to protect a schedule.

goals.description: Keep baseline riggers from being blamed, replaced, or locked out by the stoppage.
goals.emotional_stake: He cannot admit solidarity if it feels like signing his own crew's obsolescence.

episodic.summary: Orrin's sister lost a berth when a subcontracted engineered labor line underbid the baseline anchor crew.

semantic.summary: Dry anchor points must be cleared before any safe crawl-access bypass can take load.

identity.name: Teth Inkwise
identity.origin: BioElevate cephalopod dry-operation engineering lineage with ties to mutual-aid extraction networks.
identity.public_description: A many-armed dry-operation crawl technician who moves through AU service arteries like the yard forgot to lock the real doors.
private_notes: Teth treats chronic harness discomfort as operational weather: real, constant, and not useful to complain about while the room still needs air.
Teth will sabotage a bad order, but not in a way that leaves civilians gasping.
Teth understands that the yard's dependence on cephalopod labor is leverage only while everyone survives.

values.label: Leverage must not become suffocation politics.

goals.description: Keep the crawl-access bypass available without giving AU a way to erase the refusal.
goals.emotional_stake: A clever trick that kills civilians is just management with better arms.

semantic.summary: The service lung can carry Kappa's load for minutes if the harness valves are staged by someone with crawl-space reach.

identity.name: Ilya Marne
identity.origin: Aeronautics Unlimited management track, Pallas Bloom operations.
identity.public_description: A superintendent trained to turn danger into schedule language before the board notices blood.
private_notes: Ilya knows the yard depends on workers the board does not respect.
Admitting that dependence risks her career and AU's liability posture.

values.label: The yard must keep operating without a reportable catastrophe.

goals.description: Contain the refusal as an irregularity before it becomes a recognized strike.
goals.emotional_stake: If the board sees species politics in the shift log, Ilya loses control of the yard and her career.

semantic.summary: Supplier documentation says emergent worker behavior should be framed as product variance, scaffold drift, or misuse until legal review says otherwise.

relationships.summary: Lio trusts Nara's hazard sense and wants her recognition to matter without making her a specimen.
Nara treats Lio as safer than management but not safe enough for careless disclosure.
Lio sees Orrin as hard to move but too honest about safety to dismiss.
Orrin sees Nara as both a fellow worker in danger and a symbol of the labor market that hurt his family.
Teth will let Ilya believe she is commanding the cephalopod crawl routes only while that fiction keeps civilians breathing, and will hide personal discomfort until hiding it becomes dangerous.
Ilya suspects Lio knows more about the refusal than a scheduler should.
Orrin trusts Lio on safety more than politics and suspects the organizer sees through him.
Nara treats Orrin as dangerous until his actions prove whether he sees her as crew or replacement.
Ilya needs Teth's competence and resents how visible that dependence becomes during crisis.
Lio expects Ilya to protect AU first but still tries to leave her a survivable safety path.

events.summary: The fixture begins before crisis, in ordinary maintenance texture: pressure checks, dry-operation harness checks, baseline break-table resentment, AU schedule discipline, and engineered-worker accommodations that normalize exploitation.
Nara-7 and other engineered workers stop before a hazard route through Kappa-7, naming remembered injury rather than a board-approved fault.

observed_exchange.utterance_summary: Nara says Kappa remembers cutting Rell and refuses the Kappa-7 entry.
observable_cues: touches the manifold in the same sequence used by three other engineered workers
keeps body low and away from inspector sightlines

private_interpretations.interpretation: This is coordinated memory and labor refusal, but Nara will be at risk if Lio names it too fast.
private_interpretations.attributed_motive: protect workers from a known cutting path

event_effects.summary: Lio's protective alarm rises and the fixture opens the threshold choice.

scenes.location: Pallas Bloom Cluster, AU Yard Twelve, Service Ring Kappa and adjacent break-galley.
public_stakes: A routine cavity-habitat seal shift needs to stay on schedule.
Baseline, biodrone, and uplift-adapted crews must coordinate body-specific work in a shared pressure environment.
hidden_stakes: The fixture must teach ordinary exploitation before asking the reader to judge the strike.
Lio is already watching for coordinated refusal but cannot make that fact public.

speaker_local_truth: Lio knows the Kappa route has been patched too many times and that different body types notice different hazards first.
Lio can see baseline resentment and engineered-worker caution in the same room.
speaker_beliefs: A direct sermon about rights would harden Orrin and expose Nara.
Ordinary safety language can carry political work if it names material costs.
presentation_constraints: Speak like a maintenance scheduler first and an organizer second.
Do not reveal Nara's private memory unless Nara makes it observable.

scenes.location: Service Ring Kappa, near a seal manifold with cephalopod crawl access and dry anchor points.
public_stakes: A Kappa-7 entry order has stopped, and the cavity margin will not wait forever.
AU wants the event classified as equipment irregularity or worker noncompliance.
hidden_stakes: The coordinated refusal is evidence of memory, reciprocity, and collective self-defense.
Supplier-liability language can erase the action if the crew cannot hold a better frame.

speaker_local_truth: Nara has made the memory observable by naming Rell.
The life-support margin is real, and turning delay into pure theatre would be reckless.
Orrin's anchor crew is needed for any safe bypass.
speaker_beliefs: Ilya will reach for liability language before recognition language.
Teth can make a bypass possible if the refusal is not erased.
Orrin can be asked to defend safety before he is asked to endorse species politics.
presentation_constraints: Name observable behavior, not private state.
Keep the speech short enough to function under alarm pressure.
Offer a path that preserves safety and evidence.
